mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling from an already low level, the discount rate on equities declines faster than earnings expectations, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next month. The signal is confirmed if rate cuts precede positive 20-day returns and fails if falling FEDFUNDS does not improve forward equity performance.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day S&P 500 forward returns are not positive in at least 55% of months when FEDFUNDS is declining, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE above a stress threshold signals deteriorating household income and weaker risk appetite, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the following month. The effect is strongest when unemployment accelerates rather than remains stable.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not show negative 20-day returns on average, or if more than 60% of triggered observations still produce positive S&P 500 returns.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in RSAFS indicates improving nominal consumer demand and revenue visibility for cyclicals, which should support the S&P 500 over the next month. The mechanism is falsified if sales growth does not translate into positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS growth is above its 6-month median but 20-day S&P 500 returns are negative in a majority of such months, or if Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO signals stronger industrial output and capacity utilization, which improves earnings expectations for cyclical sectors and lifts the index over a quarterly horizon. The signal fails when production gains are not accompanied by market gains.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with positive 3-month INDPRO momentum, or if the sign of the average forward return is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST falls below a threshold after a prior rebound, the housing impulse is fading and cyclical growth expectations weaken, which should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next month. This is a contraction signal, not a valuation signal.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines below its 3-month moving average after a prior 2-month rise
failure_condition: Reject if triggered housing-fade episodes do not produce negative 20-day returns on average, or if the hit rate for negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE identifies genuine labor-market healing, which should support equity prices by improving consumption and lowering recession risk. Either series alone is insufficient; the joint pattern is required.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE does not outperform either proxy alone, or if triggered 20-day S&P 500 returns are not positive in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, a rising CPIAUCSL together with a still-low FEDFUNDS rate implies inflation pressure is building faster than policy normalization, which later compresses real valuations and should hurt the S&P 500 over a one-month horizon. The signal is regime-specific and should not be used outside tightening periods.
failure_condition: Reject if, during tightening cycles, the CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS interaction does not predict negative 20-day returns, or if the sign flips positive in a rolling 2-year window with Rank IC above zero.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansion, strong GDPC1 alongside robust RSAFS indicates broad nominal demand, which should keep S&P 500 forward returns positive over a quarterly horizon. The mechanism relies on both output and sales strength being aligned.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous strength in GDPC1 and RSAFS does not produce positive 60-day forward returns, or if the joint signal underperforms a random baseline over any 3-year rolling window.
